rationale: B is better anchored to Flink 2.2.0 with concrete docs URLs, implementation path, and neighboring tests. A is safer but too generic and omits the version-pinned artifacts the question explicitly asks for. B has some risk from slightly overconfident savepoint wording, but it is still more actionable and source-grounded overall.

A: No version-pinned docs URLs or repo URIs, despite the question asking for them.
Neighboring tests are described generically, so an engineer cannot quickly inspect the right files.
Leaves savepoint factory behavior uncertain without pointing to concrete source anchors.
B: The savepoint description may overstate implementation details without enough direct evidence.
Claims about queued time-trigger limits and garbage-collection handling are not tightly supported by the cited snippet.
One cited neighbor test (`CheckpointFailureManagerTest`) may be less directly related than the core checkpoint/savepoint tests.
